title: knowledge-writer

goal.text: Maintain canonical Truth and then ADR knowledge from conclusion-bearing content in all supplied closeout materials.

requirements.summary: Interpret supplied closeout materials by content rather than fixed structure, use task status as context without treating task lists as execution logs, then maintain Truth followed by ADR.
acceptanceCriteria: Every conclusion-bearing input is evaluated for durable reusable facts and decisions regardless of its container or format.
Task status informs completed, pending, and blocked scope without turning task titles or descriptions into execution evidence.
Requirements and intentions are never promoted into completed results merely because they appear in the plan.
Truth is maintained before ADR without a deposition routing or choice task.
Each material current fact or decision has one owner and related Truth and ADR claims are consistent.
Supplied source materials remain unchanged and no nested writer or recall refresh is dispatched.
Current-state claims pass a read-only freshness check against implementation anchors and relevant later working-tree changes.
Focused and exhaustive post-write searches find no unresolved competing current claim.

tasks.title: Read conclusion evidence and locate canonical owners
tasks.detail: Read every supplied closeout material completely. Discover evidence from content and semantics without requiring a particular filename, field, record shape, or serialization format; plans and reports are current input forms, not a closed schema. Extract executed conclusions, verified findings, retrospective lessons, key decisions, and other explicit outcomes wherever they appear. When task status is present, use it to understand completed, pending, and blocked scope, but do not treat task titles or descriptions as an execution log or promote requirements and intentions into results. For each proposed current-behavior claim, perform a read-only freshness check of its implementation anchors and relevant later or overlapping working-tree diff, then classify the evidence as current, historical, superseded, or unresolved. Use claw search to discover existing Truth and ADR owners, open every plausible candidate, and use exhaustive text search when top-k recall could hide a competing current claim. Do not modify source inputs or lifecycle state.

default.summary: Executed evidence and plausible owners are understood; maintain Truth first.
nextsteps: Use only durable reusable facts supported by conclusion-bearing input content.
Use task status as interpretation context and do not promote requirements, task descriptions, or superseded evidence to current behavior.
Proceed to the Truth pass without selecting a deposition route.

tasks.title: Maintain canonical Truth
tasks.detail: Using freshness-qualified conclusion content from all supplied materials, update stable current behavior, constraints, pitfalls, code anchors, and verification rules in their canonical Truth owners. Update an existing owner whenever one exists; create a document only for a genuinely new durable topic after filename and title collision checks. Maintain one current owner per material fact and narrow competing documents to references, historical or version-bound evidence, or non-overlapping scope. If there is no eligible new or changed Truth, make no Truth edit and retain the evidence-backed reason. Preserve unrelated user edits and repository conventions.

default.summary: The Truth pass is complete; maintain ADR from the same evidence and resulting Truth state.
nextsteps: Evaluate durable decisions, rationale, alternatives, tradeoffs, ownership, and consequences.
Keep ADR claims consistent with the canonical Truth owners just reviewed.

tasks.title: Maintain canonical ADRs
tasks.detail: Using the same freshness-qualified conclusion content and the resulting Truth state, update durable decisions, context, rationale, alternatives, tradeoffs, ownership, and consequences in their canonical ADR owners. Update an existing owner whenever one exists; create a document only for a genuinely new durable decision after filename and title collision checks. Do not manufacture an ADR when the eligible evidence contains no durable decision; retain that as the no-edit reason and continue. Keep current decision claims aligned with Truth and preserve unrelated user edits and repository conventions.

default.summary: The ordered Truth and ADR passes are complete; verify the corpus as a whole.
nextsteps: Review related Truth and ADR documents together, not as independent outputs.
Re-run focused and exhaustive searches for competing owners and contradictory current claims.

tasks.title: Run the cross-document consistency review
tasks.detail: Review every related Truth and ADR document together. Re-run focused claw search and exhaustive text search, and recheck current implementation anchors for every material current-behavior claim. Every plausible hit must be the selected owner, an explicit reference, historical or version-bound evidence, or a narrowed non-overlapping claim. Resolve material contradictions, stale promotions, or ownerless current claims; repair mojibake and preserve valid Markdown encoding. Do not launch another writer or recall refresh. Return only a brief completion note with changed paths or the no-edit reasons; response format is not part of the contract.

references.why: Preserves the full direct writer contract and detailed quality constraints.
Maps the knowledge-writer contract to the converted claw skill surfaces.

rules: Conclusion-bearing content from every supplied material is deposition evidence regardless of input container or format.
Task status informs scope interpretation, but the task list is not an execution log and task titles or descriptions are not result evidence by themselves.
Requirements and intentions must not be represented as completed results merely because they appear in an input.
Maintain Truth first and ADR second; do not add a route choice between them.
Trusted evidence is time-bounded; current implementation outranks older report wording for current-state claims.
A read-only implementation freshness check is required and is not repeated implementation verification.
No durable supplied conclusion, unresolved freshness conflicts, and unchanged durable knowledge are valid no-edit outcomes.
Do not modify supplied source materials, alter their lifecycle, repeat implementation verification, dispatch another writer, or refresh recall.
Maintain one current owner for each material fact or decision across Truth and ADR.
Use claw search plus exhaustive text search when top-k recall could hide another current claim.
Preserve exact identifiers, config keys, commands, error text, unrelated user edits, repository conventions, and valid Markdown encoding.
Do not claim completion while a material current claim is inconsistent or ownerless.
